data.title: Environment-Portable Plugin: Dynamic Path Resolution + Web Environment Support
data.description:
## Context

DSO plugin developers currently can only work on this project from a single macOS machine because configuration files contain hardcoded absolute paths (`/Users/joeoakhart/...`). A new developer cloning the repo, or a developer using Claude Code for the web, hits immediate failures — the shim can't find the plugin, and skills don't load. Bug 0d45-1b1e documents the breakage. Web support is bundled with local portability because both share the same root cause (hardcoded paths) and the same fix mechanism (dynamic resolution via the shim and wrapper commands) — the wrapper commands ARE the web portability mechanism, not a separate deliverable.

## Success Criteria

1. A developer clones this repo on a fresh machine (macOS or Linux) and can run .claude/scripts/dso ticket list without any manual path configuration — the shim's self-detection fallback resolves REPO_ROOT/plugins/dso (verified by .claude-plugin/plugin.json sentinel) automatically.
2. A developer opens this repo in Claude Code for the web (without the marketplace plugin installed) and can invoke wrapper commands (e.g., /brainstorm) that read and execute from the local plugins/dso/skills/ files — zero setup required.
3. When the DSO marketplace plugin is installed, wrapper commands delegate to the installed plugin skills (e.g., /dso:brainstorm) rather than reading local files — enabling dogfooding of shipped changes. Detection uses claude plugin list via shell preprocessing at invocation time. If detection fails or times out, graceful degradation to local fallback.
4. A pre-commit lint check (check-portability.sh) blocks commits that introduce absolute home-directory paths (e.g., /Users/<name>/, /home/<name>/) in tracked files. Supports # portability-ok inline suppression for documentation examples. Distinct from Enforce DSO shim usage epic's check-shim-refs.sh — separate hooks, no shared logic.
5. The existing test in test-plugin-scripts.sh that greps for hardcoded paths passes independently with zero matches across all plugin scripts. Not replaced by SC4 — covers plugin scripts specifically while SC4 covers all tracked files.
6. No changes to files under plugins/dso/ that are invoked by host projects — specifically plugins/dso/scripts/dso-setup.sh, the shim template, hook dispatchers, or files listed in plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md. Enforced via code review.
7. A CI portability smoke test runs .claude/scripts/dso ticket list in a clean Ubuntu container with no prior DSO configuration — the command exits 0. Falsifiable exit condition for SC1.
8. CLAUDE.md includes a directive instructing agents that when a named sub-agent (dispatched via subagent_type) is unavailable, the agent definition can be found as a markdown prompt file in plugins/dso/agents/ and read inline as a prompt fallback.

## Scenario Analysis
- Self-detection activates in host project with plugins/dso/ directory (medium): Shim validates plugin identity via .claude-plugin/plugin.json sentinel file, not just directory existence.
- Pre-commit check blocks documentation examples (medium): check-portability.sh supports # portability-ok inline suppression comment.
- claude plugin list detection fails in web environment (medium): Shell preprocessing includes error handling; detection failure degrades to local fallback.
- Wrapper-skill parity not enforced (high): A test should compare plugins/dso/skills/*/SKILL.md against .claude/commands/ to ensure new skills have corresponding wrappers.
- Skill tool invocation fails on false-positive detection (critical): Wrapper instructions include fallback to local SKILL.md if Skill tool dispatch fails.
- claude plugin list absent in CI containers (critical): Detection command wrapped in error handling — any error = local fallback.

## Research Findings
Plugin Loading in Web Environments (bright-line: novel architectural pattern)
- Sources: code.claude.com/docs/en/skills, code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference, openshift/hypershift PR #7068 (tried and reverted), issues #13096, #23978, #17988, #11509
- Key insight: extraKnownMarketplaces with source: directory is broken for non-interactive/web use (relative paths don't resolve, no headless support). CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR works but only when set before Claude Code starts — CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is shell-scoped, invisible to Node.js process.env. The reliable zero-config approach is .claude/commands/ wrapper files with shell preprocessing that detect marketplace installation and fall back to reading local skill files directly.

## Dependencies
- Bug 0d45-1b1e (hardcoded macOS path) — this epic supersedes and closes it.
- Coordination note (not blocking): Enforce DSO shim usage in skill files via commit hook also adds a pre-commit hook. If both land in the same sprint, hook registration should be coordinated.

## Approach
Four-part fix: (1) Add self-detection fallback to this repo's .claude/scripts/dso shim — when REPO_ROOT/plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json exists and no other source is configured, use REPO_ROOT/plugins/dso directly. (2) Create .claude/commands/ wrapper files for key DSO skills using shell preprocessing to detect marketplace plugin installation and delegate to plugin skills when available or read from local plugins/dso/skills/ when not. (3) Add check-portability.sh as a pre-commit guard with # portability-ok suppression, fix all existing hardcoded paths, and add a CI smoke test for fresh-clone portability. (4) Add a CLAUDE.md directive pointing agents to plugins/dso/agents/ for fallback agent definitions when named sub-agents are unavailable.
